title: Bifrost: Unlocking Bitcoin DeFi on Cardano — Road to Mainnet (Phase 1 of 2)

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14; Provide independent evidence for: Bifrost's smart contracts, off-chain tooling, and SDK are stated to be released under Apache 2.0. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.15; confidence 75.7%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was -0.020 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: The claims describe plausible public-benefit infrastructure, but the deterministic assessment marks evidence as thin and several high-materiality claims about security, adoption targets, revenue return, and launch readiness are mainly proposer-asserted. Under the doctrine, treasury stewardship and evidence quality justify a small cautionary negative nudge rather than rewarding ecosystem-growth upside that is not yet well evidenced.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14.
Provide independent evidence for: Bifrost's smart contracts, off-chain tooling, and SDK are stated to be released under Apache 2.0.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Phase 1 of 2 funding to take Bifrost from working testnet to launch readiness for a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge.

Deliverables: Hardening, Security audits, Formal verification, Ecosystem and partner readiness, Stewardship and economic foundations for launch, Audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in federated and SPO threshold custody modes under controlled access

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳12,332,031, approximately $1,973,125 at 0.16 USD/ADA, including a 10% refundable contingency, for a 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Bifrost custody of locked BTC is intended to be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation rather than held by a company, foundation, or fixed signing committee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): fBTC is intended to be issued and burned in 1:1 correspondence with BTC locked in the Bifrost custody script on Bitcoin and handled as a native Cardano token.
missing: Independent evidence for: The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14.
Independent evidence for: By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Independent evidence for: Bifrost custody of locked BTC is intended to be distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation rather than held by a company, foundation, or fixed signing committee.
Independent evidence for: Bifrost's smart contracts, off-chain tooling, and SDK are stated to be released under Apache 2.0.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's existing SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as a native Cardano asset." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The bridge is on testnet today under Catalyst Fund 14." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
